objective: Reframe mature semi-realistic stylization review for phone-legible evidence after the first contact sheet failed, while preserving selective living-entity aging as an explicit later lifecycle proof obligation rather than a visual or runtime claim.
next_action: Prepare any later H5 comparison as one large subject or stage per image with deliberately isolated visual differences; retain the two-lane ambient-diversity versus relationship-lifecycle aging design for a separately gated capability-free proof.
research_gate: Generated concepts may test visual direction only. Inspect actual pixels, reject anatomy and tone failures, and never infer mesh topology, shader feasibility, biological growth correctness, or device performance from an image.
authority_lane: Owner-authorized requirement recording and bounded concept comparison only. Growth, geometry, shader, runtime, device profiling, approval, promotion, engine selection, external execution, and protected-Kernel mutation remain unauthorized.
context_health: Green after a two-scale refresh. Owner phone observation rejected the first contact sheet as insufficiently distinguishable and clarified selective aging. Both are recorded without implementation claims; H5 dependent implementation remains blocked and heartbeat remains paused.

macro_findings: H5 is the first visual-inspection stage; H6 recovery and H7 promotion remain later gates.
F5 remains engine-neutral and cannot import a production asset or select a runtime.
The owner requires good-quality accurate comparison targets and review whenever fitness or creative intent is uncertain.
The verified humanoid chain is a smallest engine-neutral proof, not an in-game growth, creature, mesh, shader, or device-performance implementation.
Later organism growth must generalize beyond humanoids while one canonical identity drives coordinated phone-to-high-end presentation and simulation fidelity.
Selective aging has two lanes: stable ambient age diversity and persistent baby-to-adult growth for bred companions and children, with optional adult-to-elder progression and no old-age death.
micro_findings: Original-resolution pixel inspection confirms the Forge UI capture is clean and correctly scoped to the Forge window.
The displayed reference is a tiny three-view stick skeleton with no surface anatomy, credible human silhouette, face, hands, musculature, or detailed proportions.
The current fixture is rejected for human-quality comparison but remains valid for the exact structural H2-H4 proofs.

Three official MakeHuman documentation images were inspected at original scale and rejected because crop, incompleteness, clothing, annotations, or occlusion prevent the required full-human comparison.
MakeHuman remains a provenance- and license-promising source family, but no actual candidate asset is verified fit.
The owner selected semi-realistic anatomy and proportions with mature economical stylization and a restrained toon/cel-like shader, explicitly avoiding childlike caricature.
The owner requires growth across NPCs, humanoids, and creatures plus coordinated degradation from high-end PC to phone-class hardware.
The imported Gemini compendium is preserved as evidence only; useful hypotheses and unsupported performance, determinism, security, and scale claims are explicitly separated.
The 1717x916 three-panel fidelity sheet was technically clear at source scale but the owner reported all panels looked the same on a phone, so it is rejected as a phone review fixture.

Ambient entities need deterministic age-cohort diversity without continuous aging; bred companions and player children need tracked baby-to-adult growth, while a setting controls only optional elderly progression.
Old age does not cause death, the player character is outside this aging system, and species-authored age cues must replace a universal head/eye scaling rule.

visual_quality_gate.rationale: H5 must now compare actual rendered examples against the resolved mature semi-realistic and restrained toon/cel direction. Existing references remain insufficient, and generated concepts cannot pass as implementation or performance evidence.

receipts.provenance: Forge-owned window capture with verified PrintWindow manifest; artifact is disposable presentation evidence.
receipts.intended_comparison: Neutral human visual form, anatomy, proportions, and pose quality.
receipts.accuracy_limits: Only a 17-joint wire skeleton is visible; it contains no surface anatomy or detailed human form.
rendered_views_inspected: Front, side, and top wire panels at displayed scale

receipts.provenance: Official MakeHuman documentation image at https://static.makehumancommunity.org/makehuman/docs/main_labelled.png; inspected from disposable temp storage.
receipts.intended_comparison: Neutral full-human visual form, anatomy, proportions, and articulation landmarks.
receipts.accuracy_limits: Upper-body UI illustration only; clothing and annotations obscure the subject and lower body, hands, and feet are unavailable.
rendered_views_inspected: Original downloaded documentation image at useful scale

receipts.provenance: Official MakeHuman documentation image at https://static.makehumancommunity.org/makehuman/docs/age_0.png; inspected from disposable temp storage.
receipts.intended_comparison: Neutral full-human visual form, anatomy, proportions, and articulation landmarks.

receipts.provenance: Official MakeHuman documentation image at https://static.makehumancommunity.org/makehuman/docs/legs_modelling_2_0.png; inspected from disposable temp storage.
receipts.intended_comparison: Neutral full-human visual form, anatomy, proportions, and articulation landmarks.

receipts.provenance: Built-in generated preview from the owner-directed H5 mature stylization comparison; preview-only source retained in the captured chat and Codex generated-image store.
receipts.intended_comparison: High-end, balanced, and phone-low-poly treatments of the same adult humanoid and quadrupedal creature.
receipts.accuracy_limits: Concept image only; cannot prove topology, shaders, growth, animation, or performance. Three equal panels compress each treatment on a phone display.
visible_defects: Panel differences insufficiently legible at phone scale
Contact-sheet subjects too small for the target review context
Concept rendering cannot establish runtime cost

tiers_completed.result: Source, license, provenance, and comparison-fitness criteria screened before candidate admission.
Original-pixel inspection rejected the existing Forge wire capture and three official MakeHuman documentation images before any external application or integrated PC execution.
tiers_completed.evidence: Four rejected visual_quality_gate receipts bound by SHA-256 and pixel findings.

simulation_ladder.final_integration_gate: After visual receipts and any owner check are complete, retain exact fingerprint binding, mutation-negative checks, and the complete Forge gate before H5 closure.

unresolved_risks: No current repository asset is a verified good-quality human visual reference.
External candidates may have unclear licensing, incomplete provenance, hidden visual defects, or unsuitable pose/view/lighting.
An anatomically credible source can still be the wrong creative target for Mind Warp.
A single view can hide anatomy or proportion defects that appear from another angle.
Visual observation must not approve, promote, or overwrite structural evidence.
A concept image may look attractive while hiding topology, deformation, shader-cost, or phone-performance failures.
The exact meaning of the owner's term NNR is unresolved and is conservatively treated as constrained phone-class hardware.
Growth semantics for biological age, life stage, inherited morphology, acquired change, and creature diversity require a later bounded proof package.
The per-save extended-aging toggle pause/resume behavior is a reversible proposed default, not yet owner-tested gameplay behavior.
Age-cohort distributions and species-specific presentation curves remain uncalibrated.
Future H5 visual evidence must be legible on the owner's phone rather than relying on source resolution.
evidence_requirements: authoritative source identity, license or use authority, and content fingerprint per candidate
actual-pixel inspection at useful scale and relevant views
anatomy, completeness, occlusion, pose, view, lighting, detail, and visible-defect record
explicit verified-fit, rejected, or owner-check-required disposition
one bounded labelled owner comparison if fitness or creative choice remains uncertain
exact H3/H4 candidate, calibration, and observation binding with no promotion effect
verification_plan: research a small authoritative/licensed candidate set
inspect actual pixels and reject bad references before comparison
retain source and visual fitness receipts
route only genuine uncertainty to one owner comparison
bind any observation without approval or promotion effect
run focused, mutation-negative, and complete Forge gates
advance automatically to H6 only after H5 is verified
resume_after: The prior visual comparison is rejected. Resume H5 only with a phone-legible one-large-subject-per-image comparison; route selective aging to a later separately gated proof rather than implementing it inside H5.
verification_receipts: current Forge wire reference inspected at original resolution and rejected for human-quality comparison
three official MakeHuman documentation images inspected at original scale and rejected for incomplete, cropped, or obscured human comparison
MakeHuman retained only as a license- and provenance-promising source family; no candidate admitted
owner semi-realistic mature stylization, restrained toon/cel treatment, organism growth, and phone-to-high-end fidelity requirements recorded without implementation claims
Gemini Fractal Matrix compendium stored verbatim as attributed evidence and explicitly not promoted
owner phone observation rejects generated fidelity contact sheet 049b8dc095fe8d92834a89aa4388d4f672da9ee246d7947a27c4308e7494ff52 as insufficiently distinguishable at target display scale
selective aging design records ambient cohort diversity, relationship-tracked baby-to-adult growth, optional elderly progression, and no old-age death without implementation claims
transition: R0 accepted -> H1 intake verified -> H2 representation verified -> H3 generation verified -> H4 controls verified -> H5 source rejections retained -> H5 first stylized comparison rejected on phone -> H5 phone-legible direction reframe executing
